recurringPattern: Small rounded rectangle (usually green for success, red for error, blue for info, yellow for warning) appearing top-right or top-center, sliding in with a brief animation, containing an icon + short message + optional dismiss X, auto-dismissing after 3-5 seconds.
designHarm: The toast notification has become the default for every system message regardless of urgency or user attention requirements. Success toasts (green) appear for every saved-change operation, training users to ignore them. The auto-dismiss timing is universally 3-5 seconds regardless of message complexity, leaving slower readers with half-read messages. The position (top-right) competes with browser native notifications and is outside typical reading patterns.
legitimateExceptions: Passive confirmations for non-critical operations ('Draft saved')
Temporary status updates that don't require user action
possibleFixtures: Inline success state replacing the trigger element instead of a toast
originalObservations: AI-generated notification systems always use the four-color toast system (green/red/blue/yellow).
The slide-in animation is always from the top-right with a 300ms ease-out.
Auto-dismiss is always set to ~3 seconds regardless of message length.
Success toasts appear for every save/update operation, creating notification fatigue.
